name: Iranian Oil Bourse Opening (2008)
description: The Iranian Oil Bourse (International Oil Bourse on Kish Island) opened on 17 February 2008, establishing a commodity exchange for trading oil and petrochemical products in Iranian rials and other non-dollar currencies. The bourse was a signature policy of the [Mahmoud Ahmadinejad](/people/mahmoud-ahmadinejad) government, aimed at reducing reliance on the US dollar in global oil trade and circumventing Western financial pressure. Finance Minister [Davoud Danesh-Jafari](/people/davoud-danesh-jafari) was closely involved in its creation as part of his tenure overseeing economic policy. The bourse was established on Kish Island in the Persian Gulf, designated as a free trade zone. While hailed domestically as a challenge to dollar dominance, its actual trading volumes remained limited relative to global oil markets.

sources.title: Iran opens oil bourse, but dollar trade continues

editorial_notes: Opened 17 February 2008 on Kish Island. Designed to trade oil in non-dollar currencies. Davoud Danesh-Jafari's Wikipedia article explicitly notes his involvement in creating it. Symbolically significant but limited practical impact on dollar dominance.
